hub:
Single entry point for the CP-101 biostatistics and study-operations knowledge base. Start here to navigate the study's design, analysis, data, and delivery artifacts.

- **Governs:** the index and cross-links for all CP-101 ops notes; it carries no requirements of its own, only pointers and current status.
- **Owner:** Lead Biostatistician (study ops lead), reviewed jointly with Data Management and Clinical Operations.
- **Design and analysis** flow from the [[Protocol]] into the [[Statistical Analysis Plan]], with data handled per the [[Data Management Plan]] and oversight per the [[Monitoring Plan]].
- **Conduct and integrity** are covered by [[Randomization & Blinding]] through [[Database Lock]], then reported via the [[CSR Plan]].
- **Coordination** lives in [[Milestones]], [[Roles & Responsibilities]], [[Naming Conventions]], [[Glossary]], [[Vendors]], and [[Lessons Learned]].
protocol:
Authoritative description of the CP-101 first-in-human SAD/MAD design in healthy participants; the source document all downstream specs must trace to.

- **Governs:** objectives, endpoints, dose-escalation rules, eligibility, and the assessment framework that every operational artifact inherits.
- **Owner:** Medical Lead and Clinical Operations, with Biostatistics contributing the design and analysis sections.
- The [[Statistical Analysis Plan]] operationalizes the protocol's endpoints into analysis methods and shells; the [[eCRF Specification]] and [[Schedule of Assessments]] translate visit and data requirements into collection.
- Oversight follows the [[Monitoring Plan]], and treatment-allocation integrity follows [[Randomization & Blinding]].
- Return to the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]] for the full artifact map. Any protocol amendment triggers a traceability review across these linked notes.
schedule:
Defines what is assessed at each CP-101 visit and timepoint, by cohort and dosing day, for both the SAD and MAD phases.

- **Governs:** the visit grid, assessment windows, and timepoint nominal labels that drive collection timing; it does not define results, only when and what is captured.
- **Owner:** Clinical Operations, with Biostatistics and Data Management confirming alignment to analysis needs.
- It is derived directly from the [[Protocol]] and must stay consistent with the [[eCRF Specification]], which fields the same visits and timepoints.
- Sampling timepoints feed the [[PK Sampling Plan]], so any window or timepoint change is reconciled across all three before it is considered final.
ecrf:
Field-level specification for CP-101 electronic data capture: forms, variables, controlled terminology, and edit-check intent.

- **Governs:** form layout, variable definitions, codelists, and visit-form mapping; it is the contract between protocol-required data and what the EDC actually collects.
- **Owner:** Data Management, with Biostatistics reviewing for analysis-readiness and CDISC alignment.
- It traces to the [[Protocol]] for required data and mirrors the [[Schedule of Assessments]] for visit-form assignment.
- Conventions and handling rules live in the [[Data Management Plan]]; the build and validation of these forms in the system are executed per the [[EDC Build]]. Spec changes are version-controlled and cross-checked against the build before release.
pk-sampling:
Defines the PK blood-sampling strategy for CP-101 across the SAD and MAD cohorts, including nominal timepoints, window tolerances, and handling of deviations.

- **Governs:** nominal sampling times relative to dose, allowable collection windows, sparse vs. rich profiling by cohort, and conventions for actual-vs-nominal time capture. It does not report concentrations or PK parameters.
- **Owner:** PK scientist with biostatistics sign-off; aligned to the dosing and assessment windows in the [[Schedule of Assessments]].
- **Connections:** the analysis populations, derivations, and NCA conventions that consume these samples are specified in the [[Statistical Analysis Plan]]; concentration data arrive via the vendor feed described in [[Lab Data Transfers]], so any window or timepoint change here must be reconciled with both before lock.

Keep this note synchronized whenever the protocol amends dosing or visit timing; deviations are tracked operationally and adjudicated case-by-case ahead of database lock.
sap:
The master statistical methods document for CP-101, governing analysis populations, endpoints, and the planned summaries for the SAD/MAD first-in-human design.

- **Governs:** population definitions, estimands and analysis conventions, safety/PK summary methods, handling rules, and the mapping from objectives to outputs. It describes methods and shells only — never results.
- **Owner:** lead biostatistician; reviewed by clin-pharm and medical, version-controlled and finalized before lock.
- **Connections:** derived from the [[Protocol]] objectives and indexed from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]]. It drives the [[TLF Shells]] and the [[ADaM Specifications]] downstream, and defines the scope and triggers for any [[Interim Analysis (ops)]].

Any protocol amendment touching endpoints, populations, or dosing cascades into a SAP revision; downstream specs are not updated until the SAP change is approved.
tlf-shells:
Mock tables, listings, and figures for CP-101 — the empty-but-fully-formatted output templates that define titles, structure, footnotes, and population labels with no data populated.

- **Governs:** shell layout, row/column structure, decimal conventions, standard footnotes, and output numbering. Shells are blank by design and carry no reported values.
- **Owner:** biostatistics (shell author) with statistical programming and medical review; currently **Draft** pending SAP finalization.
- **Connections:** every shell traces to a planned analysis in the [[Statistical Analysis Plan]] and is fed by datasets defined in the [[ADaM Specifications]]. Production runs against locked data per the [[Database Lock]] plan, and the resulting outputs flow into the [[CSR Plan]]. The hub index lives in the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

Shells must be frozen and QC'd before lock so programming targets a stable specification; late footnote or layout edits are logged and re-reviewed.
adam:
Analysis dataset specifications for CP-101 — the ADaM-level metadata defining derived variables, parameters, and traceability from collected data to analysis-ready structures.

- **Governs:** dataset shells (ADSL and the relevant BDS/OCCDS domains), derivation logic, parameter and flag definitions, and value-level metadata. It specifies structure and derivations, not data values.
- **Owner:** statistical programming with biostatistics review; currently **Draft**, tracking SAP maturity.
- **Connections:** derivations implement the methods in the [[Statistical Analysis Plan]] and supply the inputs each shell in [[TLF Shells]] expects. Source variables map back to the collection structures in the [[EDC Build]], and all dataset/variable names follow the [[Naming Conventions]] standard.

Finalize ahead of the dry-run so programming and validation work from a stable spec; any EDC or SAP change is reconciled here before specs are locked.
dmp:
Governs how CP-101 clinical data are captured, cleaned, transferred, and locked across the study lifecycle.

- **Scope:** Defines data-handling conventions, query management, coding dictionaries (MedDRA/WHODrug), self-evident corrections, and roles/responsibilities for the SAD/MAD database. Sits under the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]] as the controlling data document.
- **Owner:** Lead Data Manager, with Biostatistics sign-off; reviewed each amendment.
- **Connections:** Sets requirements that flow into the [[eCRF Specification]] and the [[EDC Build]], frames external feeds in [[Data Transfers]], and lays out entry/exit criteria for [[Database Lock]]. Edit-check logic and reconciliation expectations are referenced here and detailed downstream.

The DMP is the single source of truth for data process; any change to collection or cleaning conventions is reflected here first, then cascaded to dependent specs. No participant-level data or results live in this note — methods and ownership only.
edc-build:
Tracks configuration of the CP-101 EDC system from approved specs through go-live and migration control.

- **Scope:** Builds screens, forms, and visit structure to match the [[eCRF Specification]], implements dynamics and derivations, and enforces conventions set in the [[Data Management Plan]]. Includes role-based access, audit trail, and a documented UAT cycle before production release.
- **Owner:** EDC Build lead / Clinical Programming, with DM and Biostat reviewers signing UAT.
- **Connections:** Hosts the validation logic described in [[Edit Checks]]; field names, formats, and code lists are aligned to support downstream [[ADaM Specifications]] so analysis datasets map cleanly to collected variables.

Post-go-live changes are version-controlled and migrated under change control with re-test evidence. This note records build status, UAT milestones, and ownership only — no captured data or results.
edit-checks:
Specifies the automated and manual data-validation rules applied in the CP-101 EDC to flag discrepancies at or near entry.

- **Scope:** Catalogs programmed checks (range, consistency, missing-required, cross-form) plus manual/listing-based reviews, with severity, query text, and firing conditions. Logic is implemented within the [[EDC Build]] and tested during UAT.
- **Owner:** Data Management defines and maintains the check library; Clinical Programming implements; Biostat reviews safety-relevant checks.
- **Connections:** Conventions and coding requirements trace to the [[Data Management Plan]]; check changes follow the same change-control and re-test path as the build.

This note governs the check inventory and its rationale, not query outcomes or participant data. Updates are versioned alongside spec amendments so the active rule set always matches the production database.
data-transfers:
Governs how external data reaches CP-101's clinical database, including transfer formats, frequency, file naming, and reconciliation.

- **Scope:** Defines all inbound transfers (lab, ePRO, PK bioanalytical, randomization vendor) — transfer agreements, expected formats (SAS/CSV/CDISC-aligned), cadence, and reconciliation checkpoints against source.
- **Ownership:** Data Management owns the transfer specs and runs reconciliation; Biostatistics signs off on analysis-ready structure.
- **Connections:** Transfer specifications are documented in the [[Data Management Plan]]; laboratory-specific mechanics live in [[Lab Data Transfers]]; vendor contacts and SLAs are tracked under [[Vendors]]. All transfers must be received, reconciled, and frozen ahead of [[Database Lock]].

No transfer is considered final until reconciliation discrepancies are resolved and signed off. Re-transfers follow the same versioning and audit conventions as initial loads.
lab-transfers:
Governs the operational handling of central laboratory data transfers into CP-101 (safety chemistry/hematology, urinalysis, and PK bioanalytical results).

- **Scope:** Covers transfer file layouts, units/LOINC conventions, expected visits, flag handling (normal ranges, reanalysis), and reconciliation of sample accountability against the schedule.
- **Ownership:** Data Management coordinates receipt and reconciliation; the [[Central Lab]] is responsible for transfer content, units, and re-issues.
- **Connections:** This is a specialization of [[Data Transfers]]. PK aliquot collection times and nominal sampling windows must align with the [[PK Sampling Plan]] so bioanalytical results map cleanly to nominal timepoints.

Sample-level discrepancies (missing draws, unscheduled samples) are queried back to the site and lab before any partial freeze. Transfer cadence steps up near key visits and ahead of lock.
db-lock:
Defines the readiness criteria and sequencing for CP-101 database lock(s), including any SAD/MAD interim locks ahead of the final lock.

- **Scope:** Lock checklist — all data entered, queries closed, [[Data Transfers]] received and reconciled, coding complete, and protocol deviations adjudicated (analysis-environment readiness is tracked separately, on the reporting side). Defines who signs the lock memo and freeze conventions.
- **Ownership:** Data Management drives the lock; Biostatistics confirms analysis-readiness and that [[TLF Shells]] are finalized against the locked structure before the final unblinded analysis.
- **Connections:** Lock criteria and the query-resolution standard derive from the [[Data Management Plan]]. Target lock dates are tracked on [[Milestones]]; status rolls up to the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

No TLF production runs against pre-lock data except dry-run shell checks. A documented lock memo gates the move to the final unblinded analysis. Interim safety/DSMB reviews during dose escalation are handled separately by the unblinded firewall (see [[Randomization & Blinding]]) and are independent of these locks.
central-lab:
Reference note for the central safety laboratory vendor for CP-101. (PK bioanalysis — e.g. LC-MS/MS — is frequently run by a separate specialist bioanalytical lab; it is captured here only when the same vendor provides it.)

- **Scope:** Captures the lab's role, analyte panels, reference-range source, kit/logistics conventions, and the data-delivery contact for transfer issues. Reference only — operational transfer mechanics are not duplicated here.
- **Ownership:** Vendor relationship and SLA tracked by study ops; analytical content owned by the lab.
- **Connections:** Transfer formats, cadence, and reconciliation are handled in [[Lab Data Transfers]]. Contract terms, SLAs, and escalation contacts are maintained alongside other partners in [[Vendors]].

This note is the quick-reference landing page for "who is the lab and what do they deliver"; for how the data moves and gets reconciled, follow the linked transfers note.
monitoring:
Defines how CP-101 safety and conduct are monitored across the SAD and MAD dosing periods.

- **Governs:** the integrated safety monitoring approach for this first-in-human healthy-participant study — central/statistical review cadence, blinded reporting outputs, dose-escalation support packages, and the trigger conventions that route concerns to oversight bodies. Methods and shells only; no participant-level values leave the controlled environment.
- **Owner:** study ops lead with the clinical safety physician; biostatistics produces the monitoring outputs and review-ready listings.
- **Connections:** scope and visit structure follow the [[Protocol]]; the conventions for what constitutes an actionable signal live in [[Risk Signals]]. Process improvements feed back into [[Lessons Learned]]. Indexed from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

Review frequency tightens around each escalation gate; outputs are version-controlled and timestamped so the audit trail is reconstructable.
risk-signals:
Catalogs the predefined safety and conduct signals that trigger escalation in CP-101.

- **Governs:** the signal definitions and thresholds-as-methods (e.g., categories of laboratory shift, vital-sign change, AE clustering by cohort, and protocol-deviation patterns) plus the routing rules that move each signal to the right reviewer. This note specifies *what* is watched and *how* it is flagged — never the observed values themselves.
- **Owner:** clinical safety physician owns the medical criteria; biostatistics owns the detection logic and the blinded flagging outputs.
- **Connections:** signals are surfaced through the cadence set in the [[Monitoring Plan]] and, when a threshold is met, packaged for review under [[DSMB Operations]].

Definitions are frozen ahead of first dose so flagging stays consistent across cohorts and dose levels.
dsmb:
Describes how the Data Safety Monitoring Board is convened and serviced for CP-101.

- **Governs:** DSMB charter logistics — meeting cadence (scheduled and ad hoc), the structure of blinded vs. unblinded review packages, the firewall between the unblinded statistician and the study team, and the documentation of recommendations (continue / hold / modify escalation).
- **Owner:** study ops lead coordinates logistics; an independent unblinded statistician prepares restricted outputs outside the study team's access.
- **Connections:** escalations packaged under [[Risk Signals]] feed the review agenda, and any formal stopping or escalation evaluation is run through [[Interim Analysis (ops)]]. Charter and contacts are indexed from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

All board materials are tracked with access controls so the blind is preserved and decisions are fully auditable after the fact.
interim:
Plans the operational mechanics of any interim safety look in CP-101.

- **Governs:** the ops scaffolding for an interim — data-cut conventions, output shells and the restricted package handed to the board, the unblinded-statistician firewall, and the timing relative to dose-escalation gates. This note covers process and shells only; no interim results or numbers are recorded here.
- **Owner:** independent unblinded statistician executes; study ops lead schedules against escalation milestones. **Status: Planned** — routine between-cohort dose-escalation safety reviews are continuous and handled by the unblinded firewall / SRC; this note concerns a *formal* interim analysis, activated only if one is pre-specified or a board requests it.
- **Connections:** triggered and reviewed through [[DSMB Operations]]; analysis methods and populations follow the [[Statistical Analysis Plan]]; the supporting data snapshot is coordinated with [[Database Lock]] conventions.

Where a formal interim is pre-specified, its timing, scope, and outputs are agreed before any data are examined; an unplanned, board-requested look follows the same firewall and documentation discipline.
randomization:
Defines how participants are assigned to active or placebo and how the blind is maintained across the CP-101 SAD/MAD program.

- **What this governs:** The randomization schedule generation, allocation ratio per cohort, sentinel-dosing rules, and the blinding scheme (who is blinded, how kits map to a code list). Covers emergency unblinding procedures and the firewall: the *blinded* study team stays blinded through study conduct until the final unblinding at lock, while an unblinded firewall — an unblinded statistician and an independent pharmacist — handles assignments and prepares any DSMB / dose-escalation review outputs during the study.
- **Owner:** Unblinded statistician generates and holds the schedule; an independent pharmacist manages kit-to-code mapping. The lead (blinded) biostatistician does not access codes.
- **Connections:** Allocation parameters trace to the [[Protocol]]; the code list drives kit labeling and dispensing under [[Drug Supply & Accountability]]. See the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]] for the full note map.

No treatment assignments, seeds, or code lists are stored in this knowledge base.
drug-supply:
Tracks investigational product receipt, storage, dispensing, reconciliation, and return for CP-101.

- **What this governs:** Kit numbering and labeling conventions, chain-of-custody from depot to site, temperature/storage logging, dispensing logs tied to the blinded code list, and end-of-cohort reconciliation and destruction records. Accountability must remain blind-safe — logs reference kit IDs, never treatment.
- **Owner:** Site pharmacy maintains accountability logs; the independent unblinded pharmacist reconciles against the schedule from [[Randomization & Blinding]]. Supply quantities and resupply timing coordinated with [[Vendors]] (IP depot / labeling provider).
- **Connections:** Kit IDs map to the blinded code list defined in [[Randomization & Blinding]]; depot, labeling, and distribution responsibilities are itemized under [[Vendors]].

Status is Active — accountability logs update per cohort through the dosing period.
csr-plan:
Defines how CP-101 moves from final TLF outputs to a completed Clinical Study Report.

- **Governs:** CSR authoring approach, statistical contribution timing, in-text table/figure callouts, and the methods/results narrative conventions. Anchors which shells feed the report body versus appendices, and the QC/sign-off path for stat-contributed sections.
- **Owner:** Lead biostatistician drafts statistical methods and results narrative; medical writing owns assembly; the captive programming team delivers the validated output package on schedule.
- **Connects to:** The [[Statistical Analysis Plan]] is the source of truth for all methods text; CSR tables/figures are produced from the agreed [[TLF Shells]]. Authoring cannot begin in earnest until [[Database Lock]] gates the final outputs. See the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]] for the full deliverables map.

Draft outline is staged pre-lock so only numbers, not structure, change after lock.
milestones:
Single tracked timeline of CP-101 statistical and reporting milestones.

- **Governs:** Target dates and dependencies for the key ops gates — SAP finalization, shell sign-off, dry-run readiness, lock, unblinding, and reporting hand-off. Drives the planning cadence and flags slippage early so downstream work can re-sequence.
- **Owner:** Study ops lead maintains the master schedule with the lead biostatistician; the captive programming team commits to delivery dates per gate.
- **Connects to:** [[Database Lock]] is the pivotal gate that releases final analysis; the [[Interim Analysis (ops)]] checkpoint between SAD and MAD escalation has its own readiness window; and the [[CSR Plan]] timeline keys off lock. Roll-up status is surfaced on the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

Dates are operational planning targets (e.g., lock targeted end of quarter), revised at each planning review; no result-dependent commitments.
lessons:
Running log of what worked, what slipped, and what to change for the next CP-101 cohort and future protocols.

- **Governs:** Capture of process findings across cohort cycling — transfer reliability, shell ambiguities, QC turnaround, and coordination friction between the captive site and the NA team. Each entry pairs an observation with a concrete corrective action and an owner.
- **Owner:** Study ops lead curates; any team member can file an entry. Reviewed at cohort boundaries and at study close-out.
- **Connects to:** Findings on data quality and signal handling feed updates to the [[Monitoring Plan]]; recurring file-format or timing issues drive fixes to [[Data Transfers]] specs and SLAs. Prioritized actions are tracked from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

Keep entries blameless and specific — the goal is a tighter, more predictable cycle, not a post-mortem narrative.
roles:
Defines who owns each deliverable, decision, and sign-off across the CP-101 biostatistics workstream.

- **Governs:** the RACI for SAP authorship, TLF specs, ADaM/SDTM programming, independent QC, and database-lock readiness. Names the responsible role (not the person) for each handoff so nothing falls between functions.
- **Owns:** the Lead Biostatistician maintains this note; the captive programming team and the unblinded/blinded statistician roles are described here, including dual-programming and reviewer independence rules.
- **Connects to:** all role labels used elsewhere resolve back here. Programmers and reviewers should follow the file/variable standards in [[Naming Conventions]] when producing deliverables, and this note is reachable from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

Keep this current whenever staffing or coverage assignments change; it is the source of truth for escalation paths during the SAD/MAD dose-escalation cycle.
conventions:
Sets the file, dataset, variable, and output naming standards every CP-101 deliverable must follow.

- **Governs:** folder structure, program and log naming, TLF output IDs, and dataset/variable conventions (CDISC-aligned; USUBJID/SUBJID preserved verbatim). Defines version suffixes and the draft-vs-final marker so reviewers can tell state at a glance.
- **Owns:** the Lead Programmer maintains it; deviations require sign-off per the RACI in [[Roles & Responsibilities]].
- **Connects to:** terms used in names are defined in the [[Glossary]]; ADaM dataset and variable names must match the [[ADaM Specifications]] exactly, and this note is linked from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

Apply consistently across SAD and MAD cohorts so outputs sort predictably and QC can be automated. When a new output type appears, register its naming pattern here before first use rather than improvising downstream.
glossary:
Single source of truth for CP-101 abbreviations, role labels, and study-specific terms.

- **Governs:** definitions for design terms (SAD, MAD, sentinel dosing, dose escalation), operational acronyms, and any CP-101-specific shorthand used across the knowledge base. Avoids the same term meaning different things in different notes.
- **Owns:** the Lead Biostatistician curates entries; anyone introducing a new acronym in a deliverable should add it here first.
- **Connects to:** terms embedded in file and output names are expanded here, so read alongside [[Naming Conventions]]. Reachable from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]] as the landing point for onboarding.

Keep entries short and unambiguous. When a definition is contested, resolve it here once and reference this note rather than re-explaining inline elsewhere.
vendors:
Lists the external service providers supporting CP-101 and the biostatistics-relevant interfaces with each.

- **Governs:** which vendor supplies which data stream, the contact/escalation owner per vendor, and the operational dependencies that feed our datasets. Scope here is the interface and timing, not contractual terms.
- **Owns:** the Study Ops Lead maintains the roster; the Lead Biostatistician confirms data-facing specifications.
- **Connects to:** incoming feeds are scheduled and reconciled per [[Data Transfers]]; safety-lab vendor specifics live in [[Central Lab]]; IP shipment and reconciliation sit with [[Drug Supply & Accountability]]. Reachable from the [[CP-101 Knowledge Hub]].

Update whenever a vendor, format, or named contact changes so transfer specs and reconciliation steps stay aligned with the right provider across the SAD/MAD cohorts.
